exercises.explain: Previously, we learned about experience replay, which keeps a history of past designs to prevent our Generator and Discriminator from forgetting old lessons.
exercises.prompt: Why do we use experience replay in GAN training?

options.text: It makes the training run twice as fast by skipping steps
It prevents the network from forgetting how to spot older fakes
It replaces the Generator entirely with old images

cards.title: The Ultimate Blueprint
cards.body: Imagine trying to build a bridge when every worker uses a different blueprint and works at their own random speed. Chaos, right? That is what training a GAN is like without a unified plan. Today, we bring all our stability tools together to build a robust, stable training pipeline!

A stability checklist is a structured sequence of training techniques—including paced updates, label smoothing, feature matching, and experience replay—applied together to keep both the Generator and Discriminator balanced.
cards.title: The GAN Stability Checklist

cards.body: Think of it like tuning a sports car. You don't just change the tires; you also adjust the suspension, balance the brakes, and refine the fuel mix. Each tool plays a specific role, and together they keep the car from spinning out of control on the curves.
cards.title: Tuning the Engine
A Complete Training Loop
cards.body: In practice, a developer writes training code that: 1. Uses label smoothing (targets at 0.9 instead of 1.0), 2. Paces updates (1 Generator step per 2 Discriminator steps), 3. Feeds in historical fakes (experience replay), and 4. Matches internal feature maps to keep representations aligned.
Many think that adding more tools makes training slower and harder. In reality, combining these complementary techniques prevents sudden failures, saving you hours of restarted training runs.
cards.title: More Tools, More Problems?

left.text: No checklist. The Generator collapses, the Discriminator dominates, and training fails early.

right.text: Checklist active. Paced updates, smooth labels, and replay create a steady, high-quality learning curve.

exercises.prompt: Why do we combine paced updates, label smoothing, and experience replay?
exercises.explain: Combining multiple tools keeps the system balanced from different angles, preventing one network from easily overpowering the other.

options.text: They address different points of instability to maintain a steady equilibrium.
They allow the Generator to run completely offline.
They automatically write the code for us.

exercises.prompt: A stability checklist helps prevent common training failures like mode collapse.
exercises.explain: True! Using these tools together creates a reliable workflow that makes GAN training much more predictable and less prone to sudden failures.

exercises.prompt: Combining multiple stabilizing techniques creates a training:
exercises.sentence: We use a structured stability [blank] to keep our GAN training balanced and predictable.
exercises.explain: A structured stability checklist ensures all optimization techniques work together in harmony.

exercises.prompt: Which system uses a technique from our stability checklist?
exercises.explain: System B demonstrates a stable training setup using label smoothing (real targets at 0.9) to prevent the Discriminator from becoming overconfident.

options.text: System A: Discriminator output targets are set strictly to 1.0 for real images, causing it to quickly overpower the Generator.
System B: Discriminator output targets for real images are smoothed to 0.9, allowing the Generator to keep learning.
It guarantees that training will take less than ten seconds.
It creates a balanced environment where both networks learn at a steady, controlled pace.
It makes the Discriminator ignore all fake images.

exercises.explain: By combining these stability techniques, we establish a robust foundation for building more advanced architectures. Next up: DCGAN.
exercises.prompt: What is the primary benefit of applying the complete stability checklist during GAN training?

concept: Chaining paced updates, label smoothing, feature matching, and experience replay creates a structured stability checklist. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: DCGAN.'
title: Putting the Toolbox Together
